tasks.title: Write mechanical session transcript pre-filter script
tasks.description: Create ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/scripts/filter-session-transcript.sh. The script reads a session JSONL file (path resolved from CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID + url-encoded cwd under $CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR/projects, or a hook-provided transcript_path env var), drops entry types thinking, signature, last-prompt, mode, permission-mode, attachment, file-history-snapshot, ai-title, and system, keeps user text, assistant text, and tool_use entries, caps each tool_result at ~2KB, and writes a cleaned transcript to a caller-specified output path (first positional arg). Exits non-zero with a message to stderr on missing input file or unresolvable session path. Only a deterministic script may produce the cleaned transcript; no LLM authoring.
acceptance_criteria: Running the script on a real session JSONL produces a cleaned file with no thinking/signature/metadata entry types
User and assistant text blocks are preserved verbatim in the output
tool_result entries are present but capped at ~2KB each
Output file is at least 70% smaller than the input for a typical session (per seed evidence: ~80% reduction expected)
Script exits non-zero with stderr message when session JSONL cannot be resolved or read
Script has a --help flag that explains usage and exit codes

tasks.title: Restore instance validation in validate-schema.sh
tasks.description: Add an --instance <schema-file> <data-file> mode to ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/scripts/validate-schema.sh that reuses the existing cross-file $ref registry build to validate a data instance against a named schema. Add a real --help flag that documents all modes and exit codes. Add strict argument parsing: unknown flags exit non-zero with a clear error message rather than being silently treated as filenames. The --instance mode must exit 0 on valid, non-zero on invalid, and non-zero if the schema or data file cannot be read.
acceptance_criteria: validate-schema.sh --instance contracts/seed-schema.json docs/seeds/20260604-1541-seed-pipeline-hardening.json exits 0
validate-schema.sh --instance contracts/seed-schema.json with a malformed JSON data file exits non-zero and prints the validation errors to stderr
An unknown flag (e.g. --foo) causes non-zero exit with an error message
--help prints usage including --instance mode and exits 0
Existing self-test mode (no --instance) still passes

tasks.title: Add persona/judge I/O contract files
tasks.description: Create three contract pairs in ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/contracts/: (1) refutation-schema.json + refutation-contract.md defining the refutation object personas output (including the structured error-object form for unrecoverable failures); (2) verdict-schema.json + verdict-contract.md defining the judge output; (3) persona-input-contract.md defining the input shape sent to each persona and judge (transcript path, seed path, persona role). Update contracts/OVERVIEW.md to index all three new contracts. Personas and judges output only JSON, never prose or questions; on unrecoverable failure they return a structured error object, never a free-text request.
acceptance_criteria: refutation-schema.json, refutation-contract.md, verdict-schema.json, verdict-contract.md, and persona-input-contract.md all exist in contracts/
Each schema file is valid JSON and can be parsed without error
The error-object form is documented in refutation-contract.md with an example
OVERVIEW.md lists all five new files with one-line descriptions
The constraint 'output only JSON, never prose' is stated in both refutation-contract.md and verdict-contract.md

tasks.title: Add Grep to persona and judge agent tools allowlist
tasks.description: Edit the YAML front-matter tools list in each of the five agent files under ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/agents/personas/: persona-grounding.md, persona-accuracy.md, persona-completeness.md, persona-coherence.md, and persona-judge.md. Add Grep to the tools allowlist in each. Update each agent's instruction body to reference the new contracts from T-0003 as the single source of truth for expected input and output shapes, and to assert the JSON-only output constraint.
acceptance_criteria: Grep appears in the tools front-matter of all five agent files
Each agent file references the appropriate contract files by path (e.g. contracts/refutation-contract.md)
The JSON-only output constraint is stated in each agent's instructions
No other behavioral changes made to agent files beyond tools and contract references

tasks.title: Wire to-seed verification stage to cleaned ground-truth transcript
tasks.description: Update ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/skills/to-seed/SKILL.md verification stage to: (1) resolve the session JSONL path using CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID + url-encoded cwd (or hook-provided transcript_path); (2) run filter-session-transcript.sh (T-0001) to produce a cleaned transcript at a temp path; (3) pass the cleaned transcript path to each persona subagent via the persona input contract (T-0003); (4) short-circuit the judge stage entirely when all four personas return empty refutation arrays; (5) validate each returned persona/judge result against the appropriate contract shape and retry once on a parse mismatch; (6) correct the degraded-path description from 're-run the full adversary panel' to 'rerun only the agents that failed'. The orchestrator never authors or summarizes the transcript; only the deterministic script may produce it.
acceptance_criteria: SKILL.md verification stage description instructs resolving the on-disk JSONL path, not passing an inline summary
filter-session-transcript.sh is invoked before spawning any persona subagent
Judge stage is explicitly short-circuited when all persona arrays are empty, per SKILL.md
Degraded-path option 1 in SKILL.md reads 'rerun only the agents that failed', not 're-run the full adversary panel'
Retry-on-parse-mismatch (once) is documented in SKILL.md orchestrator instructions
No instruction in SKILL.md tells the orchestrator to author or summarize the transcript
tasks.pr: gh pr create --title "feat: harden to-seed verification pipeline and to-tasks gates" --body "..."

tasks.title: Harden to-tasks Step-0a gate and add informed override
tasks.description: Update ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/skills/to-tasks/SKILL.md Step-0a to call validate-schema.sh --instance <seed-schema-path> <seed-path> instead of the deprecated 2-arg form. Gate is deterministic: exit 0 proceeds, non-zero stops and reports stderr verbatim, and if validate-schema.sh itself errors the skill stops and reports rather than improvising alternate invocations. Add a severity-scaled informed override scoped to to-tasks only: (a) quality gate (degraded verification.status) shows what is degraded then asks a one-click confirm; (b) structural gate (schema-invalid) shows the exact validation errors then requires the user to type-confirm. In both cases the override decision is recorded in the tasks artifact (a top-level 'override' field or similar) without flipping the seed's verification.status. A sanctioned override never launders the seed to 'verified'.
acceptance_criteria: Step-0a in SKILL.md uses validate-schema.sh --instance, not the deprecated 2-arg form
Step-0a explicitly states: exit 0 proceeds, non-zero stops + reports stderr, tool error stops + reports
Quality-gate override (degraded) is documented as a one-click confirm that shows the degraded state before asking
Structural-gate override (schema-invalid) is documented as requiring a type-confirm and showing exact validation errors
Both overrides record the decision in the tasks artifact, not in the seed
A note states that a sanctioned override never sets seed verification.status to 'verified'
tasks.pr: gh pr create --title "feat: harden to-seed verification pipeline and to-tasks gates" --body "..."

follow_ups.title: Establish real token-cost baseline after ingestion fix
steps: After T-0005 merges, pick any recent ready/verified seed from docs/seeds/
Run: /to-seed <path-to-existing-handoff-doc> on a session that has a real on-disk JSONL
Record the subagent token count from the to-seed verification stage output
Compare against the pre-fix baseline (~88k tokens, 16 agents, 0 upheld refutations per seed evidence)
If token count is not in the expected ~15k-30k range, open an issue documenting the discrepancy

follow_ups.title: Smoke-test validate-schema.sh --instance against the seed contract
steps: Confirm exit 0 and no stderr output
Run the same command with a deliberately malformed copy of the seed (e.g. remove a required field)
Confirm non-zero exit and validation errors printed to stderr
